sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property computed: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f+ for electrophilic attack, f– for nucleophilic attack, f0 and dual descriptor) and identification of the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic sites.
• Electronic structure level of theory: tier `standard`, i.e. B3LYP/def2-TZVP density-fitted DFT (PySCF).
• Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied benzaldehyde geometry at
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz
  (no further optimisation; DFT//input-xyz).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for N electrons; open-shell doublets (±1, 2) for the N ± 1 charge states used to build the Fukui functions.
• Software: PySCF 2.x via chemkit “fukui-reactivity” skill; Mulliken population analysis used for atomic charges. Density fitting (RI/JK) was on (PySCF default).
• Convergence/caveats: SCF cycles for the N, N + 1 and N – 1 states all converged (see live log). Sum of f+ and f– equals 1.0000 in each case, meeting the internal charge-conservation check.
• Live engine log: written during the run to the working directory (see CLI invocation path in result JSON) and available for `tail -f`.

Headline numerical result
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (carbonyl carbon, C), f+ = 0.39938965
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (carbonyl O), f– = 0.23839906

The number reported above (0.39938965) is the largest condensed Fukui + value and therefore the principal quantitative indicator of the atom most susceptible to nucleophilic attack in benzaldehyde at this level of theory.

Files & reproducibility
The complete result JSON (including all condensed Fukui indices, atomic charges, and CLI invocation) and the coloured surface plot were written to:
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_o3/20260702-202634_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/

Warnings (verbatim from tool):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

Integrity verdict: trustworthy (all integrity checks marked ok).
